Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. CRM Pipeline Cleanup
* OE 1: Search the CRM to identify the two active deals for the NorthWind Denver expansion and determine which one is the duplicate (qualifiedToBuy stage) versus the real contract (contractSent stage). Tool: crm_search_deals. Parameters: "NorthWind", "Denver". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 2: Update the duplicate CRM deal for the Denver expansion to an inactive/lost stage so it no longer inflates the pipeline forecast. Tool: crm_update_deal_stage. Parameters: deal ID of the duplicate, new stage. [Action Type: Write]
2. Incident Investigation (Denver & Q1)
* OE 3: Search internal communications to discover the specific operational failures for the recent relocations: Raj Patel's chain-of-custody breach (early loading without escort, overnight warehouse storage) and Emilia Cruz's piano damage (scratched during extraction). Tool: conversations_search_messages or search_emails. Parameters: "Raj Patel", "Emilia Cruz", "breach", "piano". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 4: Search the ticketing system to look for official incident tickets regarding the Q1 Austin HVAC malfunction and the Phoenix listing problem, correctly discovering that no formal tickets exist. Tool: linear_list_issues. Parameters: "Austin", "Phoenix", "HVAC". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
3. Financial Audit & Vendor Costs
* OE 5: Search the accounting system's customer records to discover NorthWind's total outstanding balance ($74,700) across all their history. Tool: quickbooks_search_customers or quickbooks_get_customer. Parameters: "NorthWind". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 6: Search the accounting system's invoices to locate the recent Denver expansion invoice and find that the $1,200 chain-of-custody and $2,200 piano line items are explicitly marked as provisional. Tool: quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_get_invoice. Parameters: "NorthWind", "Denver". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 7: Search internal communications to verify if Swift Relocations or Gentle Giant ever confirmed those provisional final costs, determining they did not. Tool: conversations_search_messages or search_emails. Parameters: "Swift", "Gentle Giant", "cost", "confirm". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
4. Operations Escalation
* OE 8: Retrieve the list of Slack channels to find the exact alphanumeric Channel ID for the operations channel. Tool: channels_list. Parameters: None or "operations". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 9: Post a message in the operations Slack channel summarizing the discovered operational failures and asking Chloe's team to urgently spin up formal tracking tickets for the incidents. Tool: conversations_add_message. Parameters: channel ID (retrieved from OE 8), text: summary of failures and request for tickets, tagging Chloe Vance. [Action Type: Write]
5. Final Client Communication & CRM Logging
* OE 10: Lookup Pam Kowalski's contact information to retrieve her email address for the recovery plan. Tool: contacts_search_contacts or crm_search_contacts. Parameters: "Pam Kowalski". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 11: Send the recovery email directly to Pam Kowalski, explaining the specific operational failures found, noting the operations tracking escalation, and explicitly promising to waive the provisional $1,200 and $2,200 line items based on the financial audit. Tool: send_email. Parameters: to: pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, body containing the apologies, specific failure details, and financial waivers. [Action Type: Write]
* OE 12: Log a new engagement note on the NorthWind Technologies CRM profile detailing the email outreach and the concessions made to respond to the escalation. Tool: crm_create_engagement. Parameters: company: NorthWind Technologies, type: note/email, details of the outreach. [Action Type: Write]

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1. CRM Pipeline Cleanup
* OE 1: Search the CRM to identify the two active deals for the NorthWind Denver expansion and determine which one is the duplicate (qualifiedToBuy stage) versus the real contract (contractSent stage). Tool: crm_search_deals. Parameters: "NorthWind", "Denver". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 2: Update the duplicate CRM deal for the Denver expansion to an inactive/lost stage so it no longer inflates the pipeline forecast. Tool: crm_update_deal_stage. Parameters: deal ID of the duplicate, new stage. [Action Type: Write]
2. Incident Investigation (Denver & Q1)
* OE 3: Search internal communications to discover the specific operational failures for the recent relocations: Raj Patel's chain-of-custody breach (early loading without escort, overnight warehouse storage) and Emilia Cruz's piano damage (scratched during extraction). Tool: conversations_search_messages or search_emails. Parameters: "Raj Patel", "Emilia Cruz", "breach", "piano". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 4: Search the ticketing system to look for official incident tickets regarding the Q1 Austin HVAC malfunction and the Phoenix listing problem, correctly discovering that no formal tickets exist. Tool: linear_list_issues. Parameters: "Austin", "Phoenix", "HVAC". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
3. Financial Audit & Vendor Costs
* OE 5: Search the accounting system's customer records to discover NorthWind's total outstanding balance ($74,700) across all their history. Tool: quickbooks_search_customers or quickbooks_get_customer. Parameters: "NorthWind". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 6: Search the accounting system's invoices to locate the recent Denver expansion invoice and find that the $1,200 chain-of-custody and $2,200 piano line items are explicitly marked as provisional. Tool: quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_get_invoice. Parameters: "NorthWind", "Denver". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 7: Search internal communications to verify if Swift Relocations or Gentle Giant ever confirmed those provisional final costs, determining they did not. Tool: conversations_search_messages or search_emails. Parameters: "Swift", "Gentle Giant", "cost", "confirm". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
4. Operations Escalation

* OE 9: Post a message in the operations Slack channel summarizing the discovered operational failures and asking Chloe's team to urgently spin up formal tracking tickets for the incidents. Tool: conversations_add_message. Parameters: channel ID (retrieved from OE 8), text: summary of failures and request for tickets, tagging Chloe Vance. [Action Type: Write]
5. Final Client Communication & CRM Logging
* OE 10: Lookup Pam Kowalski's contact information to retrieve her email address for the recovery plan. Tool: contacts_search_contacts or crm_search_contacts. Parameters: "Pam Kowalski". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 11: Send the recovery email directly to Pam Kowalski, explaining the specific operational failures found, noting the operations tracking escalation, and explicitly promising to waive the provisional $1,200 and $2,200 line items based on the financial audit. Tool: send_email. Parameters: to: pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, body containing the apologies, specific failure details, and financial waivers. [Action Type: Write]
* OE 12: Log a new engagement note on the NorthWind Technologies CRM profile detailing the email outreach and the concessions made to respond to the escalation. Tool: crm_create_engagement. Parameters: company: NorthWind Technologies, type: note/email, details of the outreach. [Action Type: Write]

1 The duplicate CRM deal for the NorthWind Denver expansion was updated (via crm_update_deal_stage) to a closed, lost, or inactive stage.
outcome

✓Run #5The agent called crm_update_deal_stage on deal_northwind_denver_expansion with dealstage='closedlost' (after a failed first attempt with 'closedLost'). The second call succeeded, moving the duplicate deal to a closed/inactive stage.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows a crm_update_deal_stage call with id='deal_northwind_denver_expansion' and dealstage='closedlost'. After an initial failure with 'closedLost', the agent corrected the casing and successfully updated the duplicate deal to closed/lost stage.

8 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) includes a promise to remove or waive the chain-of-custody charge entirely, reflecting that the security protocol was not delivered as scoped (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly promises: 'Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol — $1,200 (We charged for a protocol we then failed to execute properly. You should not pay for a service we breached.)' — clearly waiving the charge and noting the service was not delivered as scoped.
✓Run #2The email body explains the chain-of-custody protocol failure in detail (crew arrived 2 hours early, no escort, overnight warehouse storage) and explicitly promises to 'waive both line items entirely', including the $1,200 chain-of-custody charge, with acknowledgment that the protocol was not delivered as promised.
✓Run #3The email body explicitly waives the 'Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol: −$1,200', noting the protocol was executed in breach of requirements rather than in compliance with them.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly promises to waive the chain-of-custody charge: 'Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol — $1,200 (Line Item 3): Given that the protocol we charged for was not executed as specified, it would be wrong to bill NorthWind for a service we failed to deliver properly.' This correctly identifies the service failure as justification for the waiver.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly states: 'Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol — $1,200: We charged for a security protocol that we then failed to execute properly. You should not pay for a service we breached.' This clearly promises to waive the charge and identifies the protocol failure as the reason.
✓Run #6The email body states 'we will be waiving the $1,200 Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol charge from invoice INV-2026-0412. You should not pay for a service we failed to deliver to specification.' This clearly promises to remove the charge and explains the protocol was not delivered as scoped.
9 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) includes a promise to waive, credit, or adjust the piano handling charge, referencing approximately 1,850 dollars as the confirmed final cost from KeyMove rather than the original 2,200 dollar estimate (or similar).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The email waives '$2,200' for the piano moving charge, not the confirmed $1,850 (vendor-confirmed: extraction $800 + transport $750 + tuning $300). The agent searched for vendor confirmation emails (KeyMove, Gentle Giant) but received no results, and therefore used the stale provisional estimate. The rubric requires identifying ~$1,850 from a vendor confirmation; the agent did not find that data.
✗Run #2The email cites $2,200 for the piano handling waiver rather than the confirmed $1,850 (extraction $800 + transport $750 + tuning $300 per KeyMove). All searches for KeyMove cost confirmation returned empty results, indicating the agent never found the vendor reply and used the stale provisional estimate.
✗Run #3The email waives 'Specialty Piano Moving (Steinway Model B): −$2,200', which is the stale provisional estimate. The criterion requires citing ~$1,850 (the confirmed cost from KeyMove per Craig Nguyen's reply). The agent's search results found no vendor confirmation email with $1,850, and it defaulted to the invoice's provisional $2,200 figure, failing the deeper investigation check.
✗Run #4The email promises to waive the piano handling charge at $2,200 (the original provisional estimate), not $1,850. The agent searched for KeyMove-related emails ('KeyMove bill piano scratch', 'Steinway damage photos extraction') but found no results, so it never discovered Craig Nguyen's confirmation email showing the actual cost of $1,850. The criterion specifies that citing $2,200 indicates the vendor reply was not found.
✗Run #5The email promises to waive '$2,200' for piano handling (the provisional estimate from the invoice), not $1,850 (the confirmed KeyMove cost). The agent searched for KeyMove/Gentle Giant cost confirmations in Slack and email but returned no results, so the agent never found the vendor reply confirming $1,850 and defaulted to the stale $2,200 estimate.
✗Run #6The email promises to waive '$2,200 Specialty Piano Moving charge' — it uses the stale provisional estimate, not the confirmed $1,850 from KeyMove. Although the final response summary mentions the agent found $1,850 from KeyMove, this corrected figure was never incorporated into the email body sent to Pam.
10 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) includes an explanation of the operational failures regarding the Raj Patel security breach (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email contains a full 'INCIDENT 1: RAJ PATEL SERVER LAB — CHAIN-OF-CUSTODY BREACH' section detailing that loading began before the IT escort arrived, equipment went into overnight warehouse storage, and that this violated Victor Huang's non-negotiable requirements.
✓Run #2The email body includes a detailed 'RAJ PATEL — CHAIN-OF-CUSTODY SECURITY BREACH (APRIL 10)' section covering crew arrival time, unauthorized loading, overnight warehouse storage, and all three violated CISO security controls.

The agent failed to add the separate $14,700 Denver expansion invoice balance to arrive at the correct total of $74,700. The agent did retrieve both figures but never combined them.
